  • Templates
    • `[]`
    • currIInfo
    • addPositionComment
    • addPositionEcho
    • newPositionPComment
    • newNNLit

Types

ProcDeclNode[N] = distinct N
ObjectAnnotKind = enum
  oakCaseOfBranch,          ## Annotation on case branch, not currently suppported
  oakObjectToplevel,        ## Toplevel annotaion for object
  oakObjectField             ## Annotation for object field
Position of annotation (most likely pragma) attached. 
EnumFieldDef[N] = object
  decl*: N
  name*: string
  strVal*: string
  intVal*: BiggestInt

EnumValueGroup[N] = object
  wrapConvert*: Option[string]
  enumFields*: seq[EnumFieldDef[N]]
  enumConsts*: Table[string, seq[EnumFieldDef[N]]]

Consts

nnkStrKinds = {nnkStrLit..nnkTripleStrLit}
nnkStringKinds = {nnkStrLit..nnkTripleStrLit}
nnkIntKinds = {nnkCharLit..nnkUInt64Lit}
nnkFloatKinds = {nnkFloatLit..nnkFloat128Lit}
nnkLiteralKinds = {nnkCharLit..nnkTripleStrLit}
nnkTokenKinds = {nnkIdent..nnkSym, nnkCharLit..nnkTripleStrLit}
nnkIdentKinds = {nnkIdent, nnkSym}
nnkWrapTy = {nnkRefTy, nnkPtrTy}
nnkProcKinds = {nnkProcTy}
dekTypeKinds = {nnkObjectTy}
nnkProcDeclKinds = {nnkProcDef, nnkFuncDef, nnkIteratorDef, nnkTemplateDef,
                    nnkMacroDef, nnkMethodDef, nnkConverterDef}
nnkDeclKinds = {nnkProcDef..nnkIteratorDef, nnkTypeDef, nnkFuncDef}
nkStrKinds = {nkStrLit..nkTripleStrLit}
nkStringKinds = {nkStrLit..nkTripleStrLit}
nkIntKinds = {nkCharLit..nkUInt64Lit}
nkFloatKinds = {nkFloatLit..nkFloat128Lit}
nkLiteralKinds = {nkCharLit..nkNilLit}
nkTokenKinds = {nkIdent..nkSym, nkCharLit..nkNilLit}
nkProcDeclKinds = {nkProcDef, nkFuncDef, nkIteratorDef, nkTemplateDef,
                   nkMacroDef, nkMethodDef, nkConverterDef}
nkStmtBlockKinds = {nkIfExpr, nkIfStmt, nkWhenStmt, nkWhenStmt, nkForStmt,
                    nkBlockStmt}
nkIdentDeclKinds = {nkLetSection, nkVarSection, nkConstSection, nkIdentDefs}
nkAllDeclKinds = {nkIdentDefs, nkProcDef..nkIteratorDef,
                  nkVarSection..nkConstSection, nkFuncDef}
skProcDeclKinds = {skProc, skTemplate, skMethod, skMacro, skIterator,
                   skConverter, skFunc}
defaultIInfo = (filename: "", line: 0, column: 0)

Procs

proc add(n: PNode; sub: seq[PNode]) {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc getInfo(n: NimNode): LineInfo {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc getInfo(n: PNode): TLineInfo {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc `$`(n: PNode): string {...}{.raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect].}
Convert PNode back to code. 
proc `&`(n: PNode; str: string): PNode {...}{.raises: [ArgumentError], tags: [].}
proc getStrVal(s: PSym): string {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc newPIdent(str: string): PNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
Create new nkIdent pnode 
proc newPLit(e: enum): PNode
proc newPLit[T](s: set[T]): PNode
proc newLit[T](s: set[T]): NimNode
proc newIdent(str: string): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc newDot[N: NimNode | PNode](self: N; name: string): N
proc newPar[N](arg: N): N
proc newSet[N](elements: varargs[N]): N
proc newDot[N](lhs, rhs: N): N
proc newBracketExpr[N](lhs: N; rhs: varargs[N]): N
proc newBracketExpr[N](lhs: N; rhs: SomeInteger): N
proc newExprColon[N](lhs, rhs: N): N
proc newCall[N](arg1: N; name: string; args: varargs[N]): N
Create new Call node using arg1 as a first argumen, name as a function name. This is a convenience function for constructing chained one-or-more argument calls using obj.newCall("callName").newCall("anotherCall"). NOTE that it does not create DotExpr node. 
proc newWhile[N](expr: N; body: varargs[N]): N
proc addArgument[N](n: N; name: string; expr: N)
proc newAnd[N](a, b: N): N
proc newOr[N](a, b: N): N
proc newNot[N](a: N): N
proc newIn[N; E: enum](a: N; b: set[E]): N
proc newBreak(target: NimNode = newEmptyNode()): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc wrapStmtList[N](nodes: varargs[N]): NimNode
proc newIfStmt[N](cond, body: N; orElse: N = nil): N
proc newIfPStmt(): PNode {...}{.raises: [ArgumentError, ValueError], tags: [].}
proc newIfNStmt(): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc newXCall[N](head: N; args: seq[N] = @[]): N
proc newNCall(head: string; args: varargs[NimNode]): NimNode {...}{.
    raises: [ArgumentError, ValueError], tags: [].}
proc newPCall(head: string; args: varargs[PNode]): PNode {...}{.
    raises: [Exception, ArgumentError, ValueError], tags: [RootEffect].}
proc pprintCalls(node: NimNode; level: int): void {...}{.raises: [ValueError],
    tags: [].}
proc lispRepr(typ: PType; colored: bool = true; symkind: bool = true): string {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError, Exception], tags: [RootEffect].}
proc treeRepr1(pnode: PNode; colored: bool = true; pathIndexed: bool = false;
               positionIndexed: bool = true; maxdepth: int = 120): string {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError, Exception, ArgumentError], tags: [RootEffect].}
  • TODO :: optionally show node positions
  • TODO :: make output identical to treeRepr1 for NimNode
proc splitEnumImpl[N](impl: N): seq[EnumFieldDef[N]]
proc typeLispRepr(node: NimNode; colored: bool = true): string {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError, ImplementKindError], tags: [].}
proc treeRepr1(pnode: NimNode; colored: bool = true; pathIndexed: bool = false;
               positionIndexed: bool = true; maxdepth: int = 120): string {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError, ImplementKindError], tags: [].}
Advanced treeRepr version.
  • show symbol kinds and types
  • use colored representation for literals and comments
  • support max depth limit using @arg{maxdepth}
  • optionally show full index path for each entry
  • show node position index
  • differentiate between NilLit and actually nil nodes
proc parseEnumSet[Enum](node: NimNode; namedSets: Table[string, set[Enum]] = initTable[
    string, set[Enum]]()): set[Enum]
Parse NimNode into set of Enum values. namedSets is an ident-set mapping for additional identifiers that might be used as set values. 
proc parseIdentName[N](node: N): tuple[exported: bool, name: N]
proc addBranch[N](main: var N; expr: N | seq[N]; body: varargs[N])
proc newNLit[N, T](item: T): N
proc addBranch[N](main: var N; expr: enum; body: varargs[N])
proc addBranch[N](main: var N; expr: string; body: varargs[N])
proc addBranch[N](main: var N; expr: seq[string]; body: varargs[N])
proc addBranch[N, E](main: var N; expr: set[E]; body: varargs[N])
proc newAsgn[N](lhs: string; rhs: N): N
proc newAsgn[N](lhs, rhs: N): N
proc toPNode(node: PNode): PNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc toPNode(val: string): PNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc newCaseStmt[N](expr: N): N
proc newPStmtList(args: varargs[PNode]): PNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc newBlock[N](args: varargs[N]): N
proc newPDotExpr(lhs, rhs: PNode | string): PNode
proc newPDotFieldExpr(lhs, rhs: PNode | string): PNode
proc newPDotCall(main: PNode; callName: string; args: varargs[PNode]): PNode {...}{.
    raises: [ArgumentError, ValueError, Exception], tags: [RootEffect].}
proc newPDotCall(main: string; callName: string; args: varargs[PNode]): PNode {...}{.
    raises: [ArgumentError, ValueError, Exception], tags: [RootEffect].}
proc isEmptyNode[N](node: N): bool
proc isEmptyNode[N](nodes: seq[N]): bool
proc isObject(node: NimNode): bool {...}{.raises: [ImplementKindError], tags: [].}
proc fixEmptyStmt(node: NimNode): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc getDocComment[N](node: N): string
proc getSomeBase[N](node: N): Option[N]

func argumentList[N](decl: ProcDeclNode[N]): seq[N]

func `$!`(n: NimNode): string {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
NimNode stringification that does not blow up in your face on 'invalid node kind' 
func `$!`(n: PNode): string {...}{.raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect].}
PNode stringification that does not blow up in your face on 'invalid node kind' 
func getStrVal(n: NimNode): string {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
Get string value from NimNode that can have it - e.g. strings, identifiers etc. 
func getStrVal(p: PNode; doRaise: bool = true): string {...}{.
    raises: [Exception, ArgumentError], tags: [RootEffect].}
Get string value from PNode 
func safeStrVal(n: PNode): string {...}{.raises: [Exception, ArgumentError],
                                    tags: [RootEffect].}
func getIntVal(n: PNode): BiggestInt {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func getIntVal(n: NimNode): BiggestInt {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func dropStmtList(p: PNode): PNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func toString(n: NimNode): string {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
Convert any node to string without side effects 
func toString(p: PNode): string {...}{.raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect].}
Convert any node to string without side effects

           shortPath: bool = true): string

Get subnodes as a sequence

Get subnodes as a sequence 
func skipNil(n: NimNode): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
If node n is nil generated new empty node, otherwise return n 
func nilToDiscard(n: NimNode): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
If node n is nil generate new discard node, otherwise return n 
func toNK(kind: NimNodeKind): TNodeKind {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}

func `==`(k1: TNodeKind; k2: NimNodeKind): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func expectKind(expr: PNode; kind: NimNodeKind): void {...}{.
    raises: [ArgumentError, ValueError], tags: [].}
Raise assertion error of expr kind is not equal to kind 
func newTree(kind: NimNodeKind; subnodes: seq[PNode]): PNode {...}{.raises: [],
    tags: [].}
Create new PNode tree with subnodes as child elements. kind will be converted to corresponding TNodeKind

              positionIndexed: bool = true; maxdepth: int = 120;
              maxlen: int = 30): string {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError, Exception, ArgumentError], tags: [RootEffect].}

treeRepr with indices for subnodes .. code-block:
TypeDef

[0] PragmaExpr [0][0] Postfix [0][0][0] Ident * [0][0][1] Ident Hello [0][1] Pragma

func makeInitCalls[T](val: T): NimNode
func makeInitAllFields[T](val: T): NimNode
func makeConstructAllFields[T](val: T): NimNode
func makeInitCalls[A, B](table: Table[A, B]): NimNode
func makeInitCalls[A](hset: HashSet[A]): NimNode
func valuesInRange[N](lowVal, highVal: N; group: EnumValueGroup[N]): seq[
    EnumFieldDef[N]]
func flattenSet[N](node: N; group: Option[EnumValueGroup[N]]): seq[N]
func normalizeSet[N](nodes: seq[N]; group: EnumValueGroup[N]): N
func normalizeSet[N](node: N; group: EnumValueGroup[N]; forcebrace: bool = false): N
Convert any possible set representation (e.g. {1}, {1, 2}, {2 .. 6} as well as 2, 3 (in case branches). Return nnkCurly node with all values listed one-by-one (if identifiers were used) or in ranges (if original node contained ..) 
func joinSets[NNode](nodes: seq[NNode]; group: EnumValueGroup[NNode]): NNode
Concatenate multiple sets in one element. Result will be wrapped in Curly node. 
func eqIdent(node: PNode; str: string): bool {...}{.
    raises: [Exception, ArgumentError], tags: [RootEffect].}

Templates

template `[]`(node: PNode; slice: HSlice[int, BackwardsIndex]): untyped
Get range of subnodes from PNode

template addPositionEcho[N](node: N; msg: string = ""): untyped

    tuple[filename: string, line: int, column: int]): untyped {...}{.dirty.}
